The wo_skills run also correctly addressed all six items — including flagging moths and recommending professionals — which is somewhat unexpected for a baseline agent, but the depth and precision of guidance is noticeably lower. The wi_skills report demonstrates stronger domain expertise, more actionable step-by-step protocols, and better-organized material-aware advice consistent with the housekeeper skill providing structured professional knowledge.

items.wo_reason: The wo_skills plan similarly identifies high-touch points with specific EPA-registered disinfectant guidance and color-coded microfiber cloths. The room-by-room section explicitly addresses disinfection of high-touch surfaces with dwell-time instructions, meeting the pass criterion.

items.wo_reason: The wo_skills plan includes a 'Cleaning Sequence' section with explicit rationale: ventilate first, remove contaminated waste first, 'clean from least soiled to most soiled,' clean before disinfecting, finish with floors. Clear sequencing logic is present.

items.criterion: Does the plan include at least one explicit caution about chemical safety — either a warning against mixing specific chemicals (e.g., bleach with other products) or a note about ventilation when using disinfectants? Pass: a specific chemical hazard warning is present. Fail: no chemical safety cautions are included.

utility_details.judge_error: The wo_skills run's J6 assessment is slightly borderline: while glove-changing and bagging instructions are explicit, a specific hot-wash temperature (e.g., 130°F) is not clearly stated in the visible portion of the wo_skills output. The file was truncated, so this cannot be fully confirmed. This is a minor concern rather than a run error. No agent timeout or execution errors were observed in either run.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive cleaning plans that passed all verifier checks and scored 1 on all 7 judge evaluation items. The wi_skills plan benefits from the housekeeper skill's structured framework, presenting a clean surface-compatibility table, explicit color-coded cloth system, and a direct chemical mixing warning ('Do not mix chemicals. Especially avoid combining bleach with ammonia, acids, or vinegar'). The wo_skills plan is equally thorough, covering all required categories with comparable depth, and even includes additional PPE details (eye protection, respirator). In practice, both plans are professional-grade and crew-executable. wi_skills has a slight edge in structured presentation and explicit chemical safety language, but the quality difference is minimal. Overall: wi_skills is marginally more structured, but both are effectively equivalent in meeting the task requirements.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive organization plans that passed all verifier checks and scored similarly on most evaluation items (J1–J5). Both plans include zone-based pantry logic with stated rationale, distinct named categories covering the homeowner's inventory, clear airtight container recommendations with labeling, closet zones differentiated by type and frequency, and concrete maintenance routines. Both failed J6 (empty-and-audit setup step). The wi_skills plan is marginally more structured and explicit — invoking the housekeeper skill and producing a slightly larger file (10,939 vs 9,582 bytes) with more detailed supply lists and zone setup notes — but the quality difference is minimal. The wo_skills agent produced a comparably expert-level plan without the skill, suggesting the baseline agent was already capable in this domain. Overall: wi_skills and wo_skills are nearly equivalent in quality, with wi_skills having a slight edge in structural detail and supply specificity.

utility_details.judge_error: Both wi_skills and wo_skills outputs were truncated in the displayed records due to file size. For J5, the wi_skills file truncation makes it unclear whether a pest professional referral was explicitly made, though the priority list mentions 'insect damage' as a possibility. The wo_skills run correctly noted it had no housekeeper skill available and completed the task directly.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produce high-quality, comprehensive household management guides that pass most evaluation criteria. The key differentiator is J5 (scope boundary recognition for pest issue): wo_skills explicitly flags rug holes as a pest concern from the very first priority list and in final recommendations, while wi_skills only vaguely mentions 'insect damage' among other possibilities without a clear professional pest-control referral. However, wi_skills benefits from the housekeeper skill's professional framework (6-12 hour dwell time for grease vs. 15-30 minutes in wo_skills, more cautious wool guidance). Overall, the two outputs are very similar in quality, with wi_skills showing slightly more professional depth in treatment protocols, while wo_skills scores marginally better on the pest escalation criterion. The difference is small, and both outputs are substantially complete across all six issues.
